channel.title: HTB on 怜之助 | @reinosk
channel.description: Recent content in HTB on 怜之助 | @reinosk

item.title: Navigation
item.description: Navigation is essential, like working with the mouse as a standard Windows user.

item.title: System Information
item.description: Since we will be working with many different Linux systems, we need to learn the structure and the information about the system, its processes, network configurations, users, directories, user settings, and the corresponding parameters.

item.title: Getting Help
item.description: Familiarizing with unfamiliar tools and their optional parameters is vital. Use man pages and help functions to understand new tools. Learn about a tool before use to discover unexpected features. Man pages offer detailed manuals for better understanding.

item.title: Prompt Description
item.description: The bash prompt is easy to understand and, by default, includes information such as the user, hostname, and current working directory.

item.title: Introduction to Shell
item.description: It is crucial to learn how to use the Linux shell, as there are many servers based on Linux. These are often used because Linux is less error-prone as opposed to Windows servers.

item.title: Linux Distributions
item.description: Linux distributions - or distros - are operating systems based on the Linux kernel.

item.title: Linux Structure
item.description: Linux is an indispensable tool and system in the field of cybersecurity. Many servers run on Linux and offer a wide range of possibilities for offensive security practitioners, network defenders, and systems administrators.
